agenda_pressure: Favor residual-search metatheorems that move toward finite recognition, decidability, or complexity.
Prefer canonical automata and exact witness interfaces over local booleans or cosmetic proof objects.
Use fragment work mainly when it yields intrinsic residual or candidate-tree witnesses with later automata value.
Treat verified support and counting counterexamples as pressure toward handed or residual criteria rather than ordinary support claims.
Keep separability results only when they are sharp and reusable, not just local negative sharpening.
current_bottlenecks: No unconditional subformula theorem for outputs of residualAtomicState.
No graph-level control of every state visited on accepting residual runs.
Residual graph recognition still depends on external graph machinery rather than a closed automaton witness.
Fragment calculi still lack intrinsic residual states, graphs, and candidate-tree interfaces.
The exact same-handed threshold where residual occurrence implies acceptance is unresolved.
desired_summary_changes: Residual root states are uniformly subformula-bounded by the original sequent.
Accepting residual runs stay inside a finite subformula-bounded reachable region.
Residual recognition is reformulated as a closed internal branching automaton.
One-sided conservativity gains intrinsic residual-graph or candidate-tree witnesses.
Same-handed occurrence-versus-acceptance gaps are replaced by sharp residual thresholds.
important_verified_counterexamples: Verified counterexample to: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is ...
Verified counterexample to: Prove exact completeness of the support closure by showing `∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)`.
Verified counterexample to: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {t : String}, (Γ ⇒ A) → occurs_atom t A → ∃ B ∈ Γ, occurs_atom t B
missing_bridges: From residualAtomicState exactness to subformula control of the residual root.
From residual graph acceptance to a finite subformula-bounded reachable region.
From residual graph recognition to a self-contained branching automaton.
From translation-level one-sided conservativity to intrinsic fragment residual graphs and candidate trees.
From same-handed support counterexamples to exact residual-state acceptance criteria.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize residual subformula bounds for root and run states, then internalize residual recognition as a closed branching automaton, taking fragment work mainly when it yields intrinsic residual witnesses.
next_direction.label: finite_residual_automata
next_direction.rationale: Residual exactness and graph recognition are already proved, while verified failures of ordinary support exactness and counting-style invariants show that summary-level progress now comes from finite residual control and exact same-handed residual boundaries.

overexplored_patterns: Singleton or atom-only corollaries already dominated by arbitrary-context exact theorems.
Booleans or normal forms that only package a solved iff characterization.
Fragment corollaries weaker than solved sequent-level conservativity or handed-closure transfer.
Orientation-blind support or counting claims outside exact residual or handed interfaces.
Witness refactorings that do not add finite-search control or intrinsic fragment automata.

canonical_targets: A deeper understanding of completeness for $L$
The boundary between cut elimination and decidability in extensions
soft_constraints: Closeness to established notation and theory
themes: Structural correspondences between language hierarchies and logical systems
Extraction of logical rules and complexity

theory_snapshot: The theory now has exact arbitrary-context derivability through aligned CandidateTree, HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState, and residual-graph interfaces, together with full one-sided conservativity at the sequent level. The frontier has shifted from extensional completeness to finite residual control: there is still no unconditional subformula bound for residual root states or for all states along accepting runs, and residual recognition is not yet internalized as a self-contained branching automaton. Verified failures of plain SupportClosure exactness, flat atom-occurrence containment, counting bounds, and same-handed ordinary-support exactness force future progress toward handed or residual boundary criteria and intrinsic fragment witnesses rather than orientation-blind support claims.
